{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Okimi]] of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Okimi]] of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. - {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}}. == Notes Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}}. == Notes {{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Okimi]] of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. - {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}}. == Notes {{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Okimi]] of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. - {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}}== See Also == * [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}} [[Category:expanded pages of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Kamo clan]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}}. == Notes revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Okimi]] of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. - {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also! == * [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}} [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Kamo clan]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏}}. == Notes {{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Okimi]] of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. - {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}} [[Category:expanded pages Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}} [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Kamo clan]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Okimi]] of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."

Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō {{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Okimi]] of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Yayoi period]] / [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}} [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Kamo clan]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|12=draft}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=draft}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命|12=draft}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}*

Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} "Chigi Honki."
