Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命|12=draft|12=draft}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=draft|12=draft}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=draft|12=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=draft|12=draft}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=draft|12=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=draft|12=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命|12=draft|12=draft}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}*

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=draft|12=draft}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=draft|12=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo no Okimi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=draft|12=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}*

He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=draft|12=draft}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=draft|12=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

revision.comment: 50 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鴨王 (上古) up to rev 99569791
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 天日方奇日方命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、[[上古#日本|上古]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = [[綏靖天皇]]の時代以前か{{Efn|「[[天皇本紀]]」において、天日方奇日方命の次に申食国政大夫が任命されたのが綏靖天皇3年のことであるため、天日方奇日方命の死没時期はそれ以前と推測できる。}} | 改名 = | 別名 = 鴨王『[[日本書紀]]』。
櫛御方命『[[古事記]]』。
阿田都久志尼命『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[地祇本紀]]」。
久斯比賀多命『[[新撰姓氏録]]』
櫛日方命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[申食国政大夫]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[三輪氏|三輪君]]・[[賀茂朝臣氏|鴨君]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[大物主神]]または[[都味歯八重事代主神]]、母：[[活玉依姫]] | 兄弟 = '''天日方奇日方命'''、[[姫踏韛五十鈴姫命]] | 妻 = [[日向賀牟度美良姫]] | 子 = '''[[多祁伊比賀都|健飯勝命]]'''、[[渟中底姫命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''鴨王'''（かも の きみ/かも の おおきみ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[古代#日本史|古代]][[日本]]の人物。'''天日方奇日方命'''『先代旧事本紀』「天皇本紀」。（あまのひがたくしひがた の みこと{{Efn|「天日方」をアマツヒガタと読む場合や、「奇日方」をクシビカタと読む場合や、「天」をアメと読む場合もある。}}）の名で知られ、鴨王の表記は懿徳天皇紀のみ。 == 系譜 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「地祇本紀」によれば、父は'''[[都味歯八重事代主神]]'''（[[大己貴神]]の子）、母は[[活玉依姫]]（[[三嶋湟咋|三島溝杭]]の娘）。事代主神が[[和邇|鰐]]となって活玉依姫のもとに通い、天日方奇日方命をはじめとする子が生まれたという。同書によれば、天日方奇日方命の妹には[[姫踏韛五十鈴姫命]]（[[神武天皇]]（初代[[天皇]]）の[[皇后]]）・[[五十鈴依姫命]]（[[綏靖天皇]]（第2代天皇）の皇后）がいるという。 『[[日本書紀]]』によれば、祖父は'''[[事代主神]]'''であるという。父の名は不明。 『[[古事記]]』によれば、父は'''[[大物主大神]]'''、母は[[活玉依毘売]]（[[陶津耳命]]の娘）であるという。 * 妻：[[日向賀牟度美良姫]]（ひむかのかむとみらひめ） ** 第1子：'''[[健飯勝命]]'''（たけいいかつ の みこと/たけいいかち の みこと） **:[[三輪氏]]・[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]]の祖。 ** 第2子：[[渟中底姫命]]（ぬなそこひめ の みこと） **: [[安寧天皇]]（第3代天皇）の皇后で、[[懿徳天皇]]（第4代天皇）などの母。 == 記録 == === 日本書紀 === 『[[日本書紀]]』においては、[[懿徳天皇]]の母親[[渟名底仲媛命]]の父親として名が見えるのみで、事績については触れられていない。また[[崇神天皇]]紀では[[大田田根子]]の外祖父として'''奇日方天日方武茅渟祇'''（くしひかたあまつひかたたけちぬつみ）の名で現れる。 === 古事記 === 『[[古事記]]』においても、[[意富多々泥古]]の[[曾祖父]]として'''櫛御方命'''（くしみかたのみこと）の名が見えるのみで、やはり事績については触れられていない。 === 先代旧事本紀 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[天皇本紀]]」によれば、『日本書紀』と同じように懿徳天皇の外祖父であるほか、[[神武天皇]]2年[[2月2日 (旧暦)|2月2日]]に[[宇摩志麻治命]]とともに[[申食国政大夫]]（おすくにのまつりごともうすまちぎみ）に任命されたという。この申食国政大夫は[[大和王権]]時代の[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]または[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]]に相当するとも述べられている。 === 粟鹿大明神元記 === 『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では、久斯比賀多命と阿麻能比賀太命と分けられて記されている。久斯比賀多命は宇治夜須姫命を娶り阿麻能比賀太命と淳中底仲姫命をもうけ、墓は「泉国知努乎曽村」にあるという。そして、「大和氏文」から引用する形で、「太祁知遅若命」という別名も記している{{Cite journal|和書 |author=前之園亮一 |year=1976 |title=神話研究における「粟鹿大明神大神元記」の史料価値:「神」概念の形成と出雲神話を中心に |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10959/3058 |journal=学習院大学文学部研究年報 |ISSN=04331117 |publisher=學習院大學文學部 |issue=23 |pages=115-147 |hdl=10959/3058}}。 阿麻能比賀太命は意富多弊良姫を娶り櫛甑戸忍速栖浦浦稚日命をもうけたという。そして、「大和氏文」から引用する形で、「太祁弥賀乃保命」と言う別名も記している。 == 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天皇本紀」では、天日方奇日方命を[[大神氏|大神君]]（おおみわのきみ）の祖としており、その他に[[賀茂朝臣氏|鴨君]]、石辺君、[[宗像氏]]の祖とされる。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|30em}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=|editor=[[近藤敏喬]]|title=古代豪族系図集覧|year=1993|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230頁|chapter=|ref=keizu}} == 関連項目 == * [[鴨王 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:かものきみ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:大神氏|*]] [[Category:賀茂朝臣氏|*]]

『[[古事記]]』においても、[[意富多々泥古]]の[[曾祖父]]として'''櫛御方命'''（くしみかたのみこと）の名が見えるのみで、やはり事績については触れられていない。 === 先代旧事本紀 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[天皇本紀]]」によれば、『日本書紀』と同じように懿徳天皇の外祖父であるほか、[[神武天皇]]2年[[2月2日 (旧暦)|2月2日]]に[[宇摩志麻治命]]とともに[[申食国政大夫]]（おすくにのまつりごともうすまちぎみ）に任命されたという。この申食国政大夫は[[大和王権]]時代の[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]または[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]]に相当するとも述べられている。 === 粟鹿大明神元記 === 『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では、久斯比賀多命と阿麻能比賀太命と分けられて記されている。久斯比賀多命は宇治夜須姫命を娶り阿麻能比賀太命と淳中底仲姫命をもうけ、墓は「泉国知努乎曽村」にあるという。そして、「大和氏文」から引用する形で、「太祁知遅若命」という別名も記している{{Cite journal|和書 |author=前之園亮一 |year=1976 |title=神話研究における「粟鹿大明神大神元記」の史料価値:「神」概念の形成と出雲神話を中心に |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10959/3058 |journal=学習院大学文学部研究年報 |ISSN=04331117 |publisher=學習院大學文學部 |issue=23 |pages=115-147 |hdl=10959/3058}}。 阿麻能比賀太命は意富多弊良姫を娶り櫛甑戸忍速栖浦浦稚日命をもうけたという。そして、「大和氏文」から引用する形で、「太祁弥賀乃保命」と言う別名も記している。 == 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天皇本紀」では、天日方奇日方命を[[大神氏|大神君]]（おおみわのきみ）の祖としており、その他に[[賀茂朝臣氏|鴨君]]、石辺君、[[宗像氏]]の祖とされる。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|30em}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=|editor=[[近藤敏喬]]|title=古代豪族系図集覧|year=1993|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230頁|chapter=|ref=keizu}} == 関連項目 == * [[鴨王 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:かものきみ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:大神氏|*]] [[Category:賀茂朝臣氏|*]]

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}} {{translated page|ja|鴨王 (上古)|version=99569791|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=draft|12=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=draft|12=draft}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=draft|12=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|鴨王 (上古)|version=99569791|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}}

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命|12=draft|12=draft}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}*

Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|鴨王 (上古)|version=99569791|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamo no Ōkimi}} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]]

{{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of [[Emperor Annei]]{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of [[Emperor Itoku]].{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who became the empress of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, consort of [[Emperor Annei]] and mother of [[Emperor Itoku]]. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the consort of [[Emperor Itoku]]. Another mention occurs in the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the reign of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Ōomi|ja|大臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōmiwa clan|ja|大神氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|鴨王 (上古)|version=99569791|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese mythological figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical figures of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] }} [[sn:Kamo no Okimi]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]]

Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of [[Emperor Suizei]]{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*
