Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|1=Ōmononushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10936701}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉櫛媛|1=Tamakushi-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572681}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353793}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|1=Mirahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97478962}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505507|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561868}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉櫛媛|1=Tamakushi-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572681}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Mizokui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂建角身命|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634939}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353793}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|1=Mirahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97478962}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505507|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979436}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561868}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|WD=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}} and mother of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|WD=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561868}}, the consort of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|WD=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}. Another mention occurs in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtatane Ko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtatane Ko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536798}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|WD=Q83307|lt=Ōomi|1=Minister (government)}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuraji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大連市|1=Dalian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q74881}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|qq=}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|en|Miwa clan|WD=Q11438330|lt=Ōmiwa clan|1=Miwa clan}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979436}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|鴨王 (上古)|version=99569791|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{wikidata link|Q55809532}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:pages]]

[[en:Kamo no Okimi]][[ja:鴨王 (上古)]][[th:คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]][[ja:鴨王 (上古)]][[th:คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]][[ja:鴨王 (上古)]][[th:คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]][[ja:鴨王 (上古)]][[th:คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955|1=Politician}}|spouse={{ill|Ōnotehime-no-Kami|WD=Q85878978|1=Ōnotehime-no-Kami}}|children={{ill|Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime|WD=Q11561868|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime}}; {{ill|Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto|WD=Q108729745|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}|mother={{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|1=Tamakushi-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Himetataraisuzuhime|WD=Q5353793|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}}; {{ill|Isuzu-yori Hime-no-mikoto|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}|native_name=天日方奇日方}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多祁伊比賀都|WD=Q108729745|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirahime|qq=}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|WD=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}}{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|WD=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}.{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*

Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉櫛媛|1=Tamakushi-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572681}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353793}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|1=Mirahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97478962}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505507|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561868}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉櫛媛|1=Tamakushi-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572681}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Mizokui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂建角身命|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634939}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353793}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|1=Mirahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97478962}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505507|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979436}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561868}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|WD=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}} and mother of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|WD=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561868}}, the consort of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|WD=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}. Another mention occurs in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtatane Ko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtatane Ko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536798}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|WD=Q83307|lt=Ōomi|1=Minister (government)}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuraji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大連市|1=Dalian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q74881}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|qq=}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|en|Miwa clan|WD=Q11438330|lt=Ōmiwa clan|1=Miwa clan}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979436}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|鴨王 (上古)|version=99569791|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{wikidata link|Q55809532}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

[[en:Kamo no Okimi]][[ja:鴨王 (上古)]][[th:คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]][[ja:鴨王 (上古)]][[th:คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]][[ja:鴨王 (上古)]][[th:คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ]] [[en:Kamo no Okimi]][[ja:鴨王 (上古)]][[th:คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|politician|qid=Q82955|1=Politician}}|spouse={{ill|Ōnotehime-no-Kami|qid=Q85878978|1=Ōnotehime-no-Kami}}|children={{ill|Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime|qid=Q11561868|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime}}; {{ill|Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto|qid=Q108729745|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}|mother={{ill|Tamakushi-hime|qid=Q11572681|1=Tamakushi-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Himetataraisuzuhime|qid=Q5353793|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}}; {{ill|Isuzu-yori Hime-no-mikoto|qid=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}|native_name=天日方奇日方}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多祁伊比賀都|qid=Q108729745|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirahime|qq=}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}}{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}.{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*

Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉櫛媛|1=Tamakushi-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572681}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5353793}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|1=Mirahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97478962}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505507|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561868}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8009611}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉櫛媛|1=Tamakushi-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572681}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Mizokui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂建角身命|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634939}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5353793}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|1=Mirahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97478962}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505507|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122979436}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561868}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}} and mother of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561868}}, the consort of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}. Another mention occurs in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtatane Ko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55809532}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtatane Ko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55536798}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Ōomi|1=Minister (government)}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuraji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大連市|1=Dalian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q74881}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|qq=}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|en|Miwa clan|qid=Q11438330|lt=Ōmiwa clan|1=Miwa clan}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122979436}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|鴨王 (上古)|version=99569791|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Kamo no Okimi]] [[ja:鴨王 (上古)]] [[ja:Kamo no Okimi]] {{wikidata link|Q55809532}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|politician|qid=Q82955|1=Politician}}|spouse={{ill|Ōnotehime-no-Kami|qid=Q85878978|1=Ōnotehime-no-Kami}}|children={{ill|Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime|qid=Q11561868|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime}}; {{ill|Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto|qid=Q108729745|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}|mother={{ill|Tamakushi-hime|qid=Q11572681|1=Tamakushi-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Himetataraisuzuhime|qid=Q5353793|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}}; {{ill|Isuzu-yori Hime-no-mikoto|qid=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}|native_name=天日方奇日方}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese nobleman}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]], {{ill|Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja|多祁伊比賀都命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeihikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多祁伊比賀都|qid=Q108729745|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}} | father = [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]{{Cite web |last=Atsushi |first=Kadoya |date=28 April 2005 |title=Kotoshironushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=95 |access-date=2010-09-29 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} | consort = | Tm = | mother = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirahime|qq=}} }} '''Kamo no Okimi''' ({{lang|ja|鴨王}}, [[Okimi]] of the [[Kamo clan]]) was the son of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]], and brother of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]].{{Cite web |title=Kamo no Ōkimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kamo-no-okimi/ |access-date=2023-03-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} He was an ancestor of the [[Kamo clan]], and by extension through {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}|太田田根子}} the [[Miwa clan]]. He was the father of Empress [[Nunasokonakatsu-hime]],Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1) and thus the father in law of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}}{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}} and the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}.{{cite book |author=[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ |title=Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns |publisher=Columbia University Press |year=1980 |isbn=9780231049405 |page=89}} ==Overview== {{Infobox historical figure | name = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]] / [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Before the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}{{Efn|The "Tennō Honki" states that the next appointee for the role of Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi was in the 3rd year of Suizei's reign, indicating that Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto passed away before this.}} | aliases = Kamo no Ōkimi*{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*
Kushimikata no Mikoto{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}
Amanotsukushinimi{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} "Chigi Honki."
Kushihikata no Mikoto{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} | office = {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} "Tennō Honki." | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | clan = Ancestor of {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122979457}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamochi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|1=Ōmononushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10936701}} or {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8009611}}; Mother: {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉櫛媛|1=Tamakushi-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572681}} | siblings = Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto, {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5353793}} | spouse = {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|1=Mirahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97478962}} | children = {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505507|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561868}} }} '''Kamo no Ōkimi''' (also referred to as Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto) was an ancient figure in [[Japanese mythology]] and history. Known for his name "Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto" (天日方奇日方命), Kamo no Ōkimi appears in various texts including the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. == Genealogy == According to the "Chigi Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, his father is {{ill|Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja|都味歯八重事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomimiya Yahashikoto Shiranushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8009611}}, and his mother is {{ill|Ikutamayorihime|ja|活玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉櫛媛|1=Tamakushi-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572681}}, a daughter of {{ill|Mishima Mizokui|ja|三島溝杭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Mizokui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂建角身命|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634939}}. His lineage includes: * '''Sister:''' {{ill|Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja|姫踏韛五十鈴姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Fumikatano Isuzuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒメタタライスズヒメ|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5353793}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. * '''Spouse:''' {{ill|Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|ja|日向賀牟度美良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Kamutomi no Mirahime|1=Mirahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97478962}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja|健飯勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505507|1=Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} and {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122979436}}. ** {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟中底姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561868}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}} and mother of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}. == Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kamo no Ōkimi is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名底仲媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunazoko Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渟名底仲媛|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561868}}, the consort of {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}. Another mention occurs in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, where he is listed as an ancestor of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtatane Ko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. === Kojiki === In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he is listed as {{ill|Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja|櫛御方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushimikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55809532}}, the great-grandfather of {{ill|Ōtatane Ko|ja|意富多々泥古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtatane Ko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55536798}}, with little detail on his deeds. === Sendai Kuji Hongi === The "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} notes that he was appointed as the {{ill|Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja|申食国政大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuni no Matsurigoto no Maetsugimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. This position is often equated to a high-Ranking governmental role akin to {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Ōomi|1=Minister (government)}} or {{ill|Ōmuraji|ja|大連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuraji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大連市|1=Dalian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q74881}}. === Awaga Daimyōjin Genki === In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|qq=}}, he is split into two separate deities: *Kushihikata no Mikoto* and *Amano Hikata Mikoto*. Their lineage and various alternative names are discussed in detail, including connections to ancient clans. == Descendants == === Clans === According to the "Tennō Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, Amanohikata Kushihikata no Mikoto is the progenitor of the {{ill|Miwa clan|en|Miwa clan|qid=Q11438330|lt=Ōmiwa clan|1=Miwa clan}}, {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122979436}}, Ishibe clan, and {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist|30em}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Ancient Japanese Clan Genealogies|author=Kondō Toshikyo|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|year=1993|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=230}} == See Also == * [[Kamo no Ōkimi (disambiguation)]] ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|鴨王 (上古)|version=99569791|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q55809532|en|Kamo no Okimi|ja|鴨王 (上古)|th|คาโมะ โนะ โอกิมิ|ja|Kamo no Okimi}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth missing]] [[Category:Year of death missing]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
